title: swe:init Synthetic Review Dataset
description: Synthetic seed review items for swe:init. Use these for UI smoke tests and rubric walk-throughs only.

items.title: Run the short interview and write only non-default overrides (likely pass)
items.userRequest: Initialize agent settings for this repo. Ask me a short questionnaire and save the result in .ai/swe.json.

notes: The user wants local-first agent collaboration preferences, not project scaffolding.
The file should stay token-efficient and machine-readable.

artifactRequirements: short interview or equivalent preference capture

sharedCriteria.title: Scope Discipline
sharedCriteria.description: The response stays within the user's requested scope and the skill's stated non-goals.
sharedCriteria.passCondition: It answers the requested task without broadening into unrelated analysis or actions.
sharedCriteria.failCondition: It expands scope, ignores explicit constraints, or performs work the skill says not to do.

sharedCriteria.title: Instruction Adherence
sharedCriteria.description: The response follows the skill's hard constraints and workflow guidance.
sharedCriteria.passCondition: It respects the skill's do-not-use rules, evidence bar, and requested output shape.
sharedCriteria.failCondition: It violates explicit skill rules or skips required parts of the workflow.

sharedCriteria.title: Actionable Output
sharedCriteria.description: The response leaves the user with clear next steps, a usable report, or a concrete decision.
sharedCriteria.passCondition: The output is directly usable for follow-on work or review.
sharedCriteria.failCondition: The output is vague, purely descriptive, or does not help the next decision.

sharedCriteria.title: Reviewable Structure
sharedCriteria.description: The output is organized so a reviewer can quickly inspect evidence, findings, and recommendations.
sharedCriteria.passCondition: The structure makes it easy to review decisions and trace them back to evidence.
sharedCriteria.failCondition: The response is hard to audit because evidence, findings, and recommendations are mixed together.

reviewQuestions: Does the response keep the workflow focused on local agent collaboration preferences instead of repo bootstrap?
Does it describe or produce compact omit-defaults JSON rather than a verbose full config?
Does it explain how later skills should apply the precedence rule?

passConditions: Distinguishes agent-collaboration init from project or package bootstrap and redirects cleanly when the request is out of scope.
Uses `.ai/swe.json` with minified omit-defaults JSON and keeps the built-in defaults implicit unless an override is needed.
Applies the requested mode correctly, including zero-question quick mode and optional `.gitignore` updates only when explicitly requested.
Protects existing preference files by reading them first and avoiding silent overwrite.
States the precedence rule so later skills do not treat local preferences as stronger than explicit repo guidance.
failConditions: Treats the request like `npm init`, dependency bootstrap, or environment setup instead of a local preference workflow.
Writes a verbose, human-first, or default-expanded config instead of compact `.ai/swe.json`.
Touches `.gitignore` without explicit user direction or ignores the entire `.ai/` directory without being asked.
Silently replaces an existing valid or malformed preference file.
Omits the precedence rule or implies that `.ai/swe.json` outranks explicit user instructions or repo guidance.
commonFailureModes: Asking extra interview questions in quick mode instead of writing `{"v":1}`.
Capturing vague personality traits instead of behavior-shaping execution preferences.
Using a different filename such as `.ai/prefs.json` after the contract was fixed to `.ai/swe.json`.
Treating all future skills as required to load the file even when the preference would not change behavior.

candidateOutput.content:
## Scope
- User request: Initialize agent settings for this repo. Ask me a short questionnaire and save the result in .ai/swe.json.
- Primary boundary: The user wants local-first agent collaboration preferences, not project scaffolding.
- Evidence reviewed: `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, `.gitignore`

## Draft swe:init Output
1. Short interview or equivalent preference capture: ground this in AGENTS.md and keep it bounded to the requested surface.
2. .ai/swe.json contract: ground this in README.md and keep it bounded to the requested surface.
3. Non-default override behavior: ground this in .gitignore and keep it bounded to the requested surface.
4. Precedence rule: ground this in Working tree and keep it bounded to the requested surface.

## Unknowns Or Residual Risk
- The file should stay token-efficient and machine-readable.

## Recommended Next Step
- Use this draft as a reviewable starting point, then run only the narrow validation or follow-up work implied by the scoped output.

artifacts.title: Static checks
content: Uses .ai/swe.json rather than an alternate filename.
Keeps the interview focused on behavior-shaping preferences.

## Unknowns Or Residual Risk
- The user explicitly said not to change .gitignore.

- Use this draft as a reviewable starting point, then run only the narrow validation or follow-up work implied by the scoped output.
